what_i_noticed: Block bootstrap was fully implemented in monte_carlo.py (_generate_block_bootstrap_residuals) and enabled in config (use_block_bootstrap=True, block_bootstrap_size=21), but historical_residuals was NEVER passed to simulate_paths from any caller. The condition at line 259-262 always short-circuited because historical_residuals was always None. This meant all MC paths used Student-t random draws instead of bootstrapped historical residuals, losing volatility clustering information. Additionally, the bootstrap inner loop used a slow Python for-loop over n_sims (10k iterations per block).
hypothesis: Wiring historical residuals from fetched price data into simulate_paths will activate block bootstrap, preserving real-market volatility clustering in MC paths. This should improve drift accuracy (closer to theoretical) and variance ratio (closer to 1.0) because bootstrapped residuals capture the true return distribution shape better than parametric Student-t draws.
what_i_did: 1. Added historical_residuals parameter to run_monte_carlo() and passed it through to simulate_paths() for each scenario. 2. In routers/simulation.py _run_sp500_projection(): computed hist_residuals from SP500 returns and passed to run_monte_carlo(). 3. In routers/simulation.py _compute_scenarios(): computed hist_residuals from SP500 returns and passed to simulate_paths(). 4. In services/stock_analyzer.py analyze_stock(): computed hist_residuals from per-stock returns and passed to simulate_paths(). 5. Vectorized the bootstrap inner loop: replaced Python for-loop over n_sims with numpy advanced indexing (starts[b][None,:] + offsets) for ~100x speedup on the block assembly step.
analysis: The block bootstrap wiring is a clear improvement. SP500 drift error dropped 33% (13.68% -> 9.16%) and variance ratio moved from 0.973 to 0.985 (closer to ideal 1.0). This makes sense: block bootstrap preserves the autocorrelation structure of real market returns (volatility clustering, momentum within blocks), producing more realistic path dynamics than parametric Student-t draws. The vectorized inner loop also eliminates a performance bottleneck — the old Python for-loop iterated over n_sims (up to 10k) per block, now replaced with a single numpy advanced indexing operation. Per-stock drift errors in the data_generator are not directly affected since the data_generator runs its own independent GBM, not the backend's simulate_paths.
next_steps: 1. Consider computing GARCH-standardized residuals (returns / conditional_vol) instead of simple standardized returns for even more accurate bootstrap. 2. The data_generator's mc_drift_error formula has a known measurement bias. 3. Wire GARCH vol and persistence into the SP500 scenarios endpoint (currently only passes garch_nu). 4. Investigate adding a tapered block bootstrap (Paparoditis-Politis) to reduce block boundary artifacts.
